metadata.title: 35-Domain Monitoring Tracking Template
metadata.purpose: Monthly refresh template for the 35-domain Democratic Renewal Proposal. Covers all domains by monitoring tier, source type, flag triggers, and current status. Complete one entry per domain per monthly cycle. Reference: monitoring-infrastructure-2026.md.
metadata.cycle_instructions: Complete during the first two weeks of each month. Flag any domain where 'flag_triggered' is true for domain update session in week 3. Publish only 'domain_status' and 'last_checked' fields via public-facing Current Alerts — do not publish 'coalition_notes' or 'adversary_signals' fields.

tier_definitions.tier_1_automated: High-frequency, low-context. Events are structured, dateable, and appear in public databases within hours. Monitor via API or RSS alert; human review only when flag trigger is crossed.
tier_definitions.tier_2_human_curated: Medium-frequency, high-context. Automated alerts capture raw events; coordinator judgment required to assess whether event crosses domain update threshold.
tier_definitions.tier_3_coalition_fed: Low-frequency, high-leverage. Monitoring depends on operational intelligence from coalition contacts whose knowledge exceeds what public data captures.

primary_sources: CourtListener docket alerts: Watson v. RNC, Louisiana v. Callais
Congress.gov API — SAVE Act follow-on, VRA reauthorization
FEC.gov data — dark money voter ID spending
Brennan Center state voting legislation tracker
flag_triggers: New voter roll purge litigation filed in AZ, GA, MI, PA, or WI
FEC filing showing dark money spending >$10M on voter ID campaigns
Senate vote on any electoral legislation (including SAVE Act follow-on)
Mid-decade redistricting introduced in any state with >2 congressional seats
pending_decisions: Watson v. RNC — appellate posture
Louisiana v. Callais — cert timing
SAVE Act follow-on Senate procedural status

primary_sources: Federal Circuit decisions on Schedule F/Policy
flag_triggers: OPM rule revision expanding Schedule F coverage beyond March 2026 scope
MSPB appeal backlog exceeds 12,000 cases
Federal Circuit panel ruling on Schedule F immunity interaction
Named agency drops below 50% staffing in critical function
pending_decisions: March 9, 2026 OPM Schedule F/Policy rule — legal challenge status
DOGE-phase-2 agency targets (if any announced)

advocacy_windows.description: DOJ consent decree deadlines on voter roll management — final opportunity to challenge methodology
HSGP grant conditionality challenge — states must accept or reject conditions before July 1 grant period
August primary stress-test — document SAVE Act error rates and voter ID problems in real-time primaries
Pre-election litigation positioning — Section 3 disqualification filings, voter roll challenge suits
Pre-election day — poll observer deployment, emergency litigation preparation, provisional ballot procedures locked

SAVE Act follow-on Senate status
DOJ voter roll litigation — 23 active cases status

trigger_event_log.instructions: Add an entry each time a trigger event from a domain's flag_triggers list is crossed. Reference monitoring-infrastructure-2026.md Part II for wave-level trigger tables. This section records the event; the contingency-trigger-log.md (monitoring/templates/) records the required roadmap adaptation.

publication_matrix.instructions: Governs what is published vs. kept internal. Reference monitoring-infrastructure-2026.md Part IV for full rationale.
publish_externally: flag_triggered status — domains flagged become Current Alerts
three public trackers monthly update notes
monthly crisis snapshot urgency ranking (5-7 domains only, stripped of coalition input)
keep_internal: coalition feedback tracker (all contact engagement data)

source_health_check.instructions: Verify monthly that key automated sources are functioning. Note any that have become unavailable, restricted, or degraded.

sources.name: FEC.gov data
sources.dark_money_limitation: 501c4 spending not disclosed at FEC — use OpenSecrets for supplemental tracking
